channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Barcelona beat Lyon 4-0 to claim Women’s Champions League
item.description: Barcelona claim their fourth trophy of the season by beating Lyon 4-0 in Norway in the Women's Champions League final.

item.title: ‘United States of the Middle East?’: Trump posts US flag covering Iran
item.description: Latest Truth Social post comes amid 'delicate diplomacy', expert says, as US and Iran indicate progress in talks.

item.title: Morocco king pardons jailed Senegal football fans for humanitarian reasons
item.description: Morocco jailed 18 Senegal fans following fan disturbances at the Africa Cup of Nations final in January.

item.title: Thousands march in Madrid demanding Spanish PM quit over graft allegations
item.description: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez maintains the cases against his family and entourage are politically motivated.

item.title: Hull beat Boro in world’s most lucrative game to reach Premier League
item.description: Hull City beat Middlesbrough 1-0 in Championship playoff final at Wembley to seal promotion and ease spy-gate scandal.

item.title: Thousands rally in Taiwan to boost defence spending amid China tensions
item.description: Thousands rally in Taipei backing higher defence spending after US pauses $14 billion arms sale to Taiwan.

item.title: Spanish police beat and detain Gaza flotilla activists at airport
item.description: Spanish police clashed with members of the Global Sumud Flotilla and supporters at Bilbao Airport.

item.title: Iran sends ‘mixed’ messages as Pakistani mediators depart Tehran
item.description: Pakistan’s army chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, has reportedly departed Iran after holding multiple meetings.

item.title: Overnight Israeli strikes on Gaza leave behind heavy destruction
item.description: Overnight Israeli strikes devastated the Nuseirat and Bureij refugee camps in central Gaza despite an ongoing ceasefire.

item.title: US Secretary of State kicks off India visit, invites Modi to White House
item.description: US-Israel war with Iran among issues looming large as Rubio expected to seek to mend in ties with New Delhi.

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns vs AS FAR Rabat: CAF Champions League final preview
item.description: South Africa's Mamelodi Sundowns are seeking to end a below-par season on a high when they face AS FAR Rabat in Morocco.

item.title: Israeli forces arrest two children in occupied West Bank village
item.description: Israeli soldiers arrest two Palestinian children during a raid on al-Mughayyir village in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: More than 500 children die in measles outbreak in Bangladesh
item.description: Most cases recorded by doctors among children aged between six months and five years.

item.title: Five police officers, 13 year-old boy killed in Israeli attack on Gaza
item.description: Attack on a police post in northern Gaza comes as dozens of people were wounded in Israeli attacks in the past 48 hours.

item.title: French Open has ‘positive’ meeting with players over tennis dispute
item.description: Roland Garros director discusses greater revenue sharing with player representatives after boycott-impacted media day.

item.title: Rising global costs threaten Mexico’s production costs and food stability
item.description: Sharp price hikes for food staples are forcing Mexicans to tighten budgets and cut back consumption.

item.title: Turkish and Syrian officials detain 10 individuals with alleged ISIL ties
item.description: Those arrested reportedly took part in what officials described as 'terrorist attacks' in Turkiye.

item.title: France bans far-right Israeli minister Ben-Gvir over abuse of activists
item.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said France would not tolerate its nationals being threatened or intimidated.

item.title: Israeli air strike pounds southern Lebanon leaving many injured
item.description: An Israeli air strike has hit the Al-Baqbouq area north of Tyre in southern Lebanon.

item.title: The pollution that outlives war
item.description: Long after fighting is over, the toxic leftovers of war continue to poison communities and the environment.

item.title: DR Congo will not change World Cup preparations despite US Ebola warning
item.description: World Cup cohosts United States have warned Democratic Republic of the Congo team to isolate due to Ebola fears.

item.title: F1 legend Prost suffers head injury in alleged robbery at Swiss home
item.description: Former Formula 1 champion Alain Prost reportedly injured as police investigate robbery at family home in Switzerland.

item.title: Massive fire engulfs residential area in the Philippine capital
item.description: A massive fire is tearing through a densely-populated neighbourhood in Manila.

item.title: Cockroach Janta Party’s founder says Indian government took website down
item.description: Group’s creator says his Instagram page was also hacked, along with the movement’s account.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Haiti fans feel excluded from first tournament since 1974
item.description: Expensive tickets and transport prices and a US travel ban mean few Haitians will be able to attend the matches.
